back_translation: And it happened afterward, that Absalom son of David had a beautiful sister, and her name was Tamar; and Amnon son of David loved her.
And Amnon was distressed to the point of illness because of Tamar his sister, for she was a virgin; and it seemed difficult in the eyes of Amnon to do anything to her.
And Amnon had a friend, and his name was Jonadab son of Shimeah, brother of David; and Jonadab was a very shrewd man.
And he said to him, 'Why are you, son of the king, thus weak morning by morning? Will you not tell me?' And Amnon said to him, 'Tamar the sister of Absalom my brother — I love her.'

And Jonadab said to him, 'Lie on your bed and feign illness; and your father will come to see you, and you shall say to him, Please let Tamar my sister come and feed me bread, and let her prepare the food before my eyes that I may see and eat from her hand.'
And Amnon lay down and feigned illness; and the king came to see him, and Amnon said to the king, 'Please let Tamar my sister come and make before my eyes two cakes, and I will eat from her hand.'
And David sent to Tamar at the house, saying, 'Go please to the house of Amnon your brother, and prepare for him the food.'
And Tamar went to the house of Amnon her brother, who was lying down; and she took the dough and kneaded it and made cakes before his eyes, and baked the cakes.
And she took the pan and poured it out before him, but he refused to eat. And Amnon said, 'Send out every man from me.' And every man went out from him.
And Amnon said to Tamar, 'Bring the food to the chamber, and I will eat from your hand.' And Tamar took the cakes that she had made and brought them to Amnon her brother in the chamber.
And she brought them to him to eat, and he took hold of her and said to her, 'Come, lie with me, my sister.'
And she said to him, 'No, my brother, do not violate me, for thus shall not be done in Israel; do not do this disgraceful thing.
And I — where would I take my reproach? And you would be as one of the fools in Israel. And now, please speak to the king, for he will not withhold me from you.'
And he refused to listen to her voice, and was stronger than she, and violated her and lay with her.
And Amnon hated her with a very great hatred — for greater was the hatred with which he hated her than the love with which he had loved her — and Amnon said to her, 'Get up, go.'
And she said to him, 'No, because of the great evil — greater than the other you have done to me — to send me away.' But he would not listen to her.
And he called his young man, his attendant, and said, 'Send out please this from me outside, and bolt the door after her.'
And upon her was a robe of many colors, because thus were dressed the king's virgin daughters with robes. And his attendant brought her outside, and bolted the door after her.
And Tamar put ashes upon her head, and the robe of many colors that was upon her she tore, and put her hand upon her head, and went away, going and crying out.

And Absalom her brother said to her, 'Has Amnon your brother been with you? And now, my sister, be silent — he is your brother; do not set your heart on this thing.' And Tamar lived, and a desolate woman, in the house of Absalom her brother.
And King David heard all these words, and he was very angry.
And Absalom did not speak with Amnon from bad to good, because Absalom hated Amnon concerning the thing that he had violated Tamar his sister.
And it happened after two years of days, that there were sheepshearers for Absalom in Baal-hazor which is near Ephraim; and Absalom invited all the sons of the king.
And Absalom came to the king and said, 'Behold please, there are sheepshearers for your servant. Let the king please go, and his servants, with your servant.'
And the king said to Absalom, 'No, my son, let us not please all of us go, that we may not be a burden upon you.' And he pressed him, but he was not willing to go, and blessed him.
And Absalom said, 'And if not, let Amnon my brother please go with us.' And the king said to him, 'Why should he go with you?'
And Absalom pressed him, and he sent with him Amnon and all the sons of the king.

And Absalom commanded his young men, saying, 'See please when the heart of Amnon is merry with wine, and I say to you, Strike Amnon — then put him to death. Do not fear; have I not commanded you? Be strong and be sons of valor.'
And the young men of Absalom did to Amnon as Absalom commanded; and all the sons of the king arose, and each man mounted on his mule and fled.
And it happened, while they were on the way, that the report came to David, saying, 'Absalom has struck all the sons of the king, and not one of them remains.'
And the king arose and tore his garments, and lay on the ground; and all his servants were standing by with torn garments.

And Jonadab son of Shimeah brother of David answered and said, 'Let not my lord say that all the young men, the sons of the king, were put to death — for Amnon alone is dead. For by the mouth of Absalom this was determined from the day of his violating Tamar his sister.
And now, let not my lord the king set anything to his heart, saying, All the sons of the king are dead — for Amnon alone is dead.'
And Absalom fled. And the young man, the watchman, lifted up his eyes and saw, and behold, many people were coming from a road behind him, from the side of the mountain.
And Jonadab said to the king, 'Behold, the sons of the king have come; according to the word of your servant, so it is.'
And it happened, when he finished speaking, that behold, the sons of the king came in, and they lifted up their voice and wept; and also the king and all his servants wept a very great weeping.
And Absalom fled and went to Talmai son of Ammihud, king of Geshur. And he mourned over his son all the days.
And Absalom fled and went to Geshur, and was there three years.
And David the king longed to go out to Absalom, for he was comforted concerning Amnon, that he was dead.
